disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden loss of consciousness that occurred while seated at rest, with no warning symptoms beforehand (no nausea, sweating, light-headedness or visual greying)
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when it happened?
Did you have any warning before you fainted?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was just sitting at the table, completely still. There was no warning — no dizziness or feeling sick — I just went down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A brief, intense, fast heart flutter felt immediately before losing consciousness
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice anything with your heart just before?
What was the very last thing you felt before blacking out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Right before I blacked out my heart fluttered really hard and fast for a second — then everything went black.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid, complete recovery after the episode with no prolonged confusion, no tongue-biting and no incontinence
trigger_question_examples: How did you feel when you came round?
Were you confused for long, or did you bite your tongue or wet yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I came round pretty quickly and knew where I was — I wasn't confused for long. I didn't bite my tongue and there was no wetting myself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Frequent unprovoked palpitations or awareness of a racing heartbeat over the preceding six months, previously dismissed
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any episodes of your heart racing or fluttering before this?
Over the last few months, have you noticed your heartbeat doing anything unusual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you ask — yeah, I've had my heart race or flutter on and off for about six months. I just ignored it because it always passed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of a brother who had a sudden cardiac arrest at age 28
trigger_question_examples: Is there any history of heart problems or sudden death in your family?
Has anyone in your family died young or unexpectedly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My brother had a sudden cardiac arrest at 28. That's why I'm so frightened about this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of a mother diagnosed with atrial fibrillation in her 30s
trigger_question_examples: Any other family members with heart rhythm problems?
Does anyone in your family have an irregular heartbeat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My mum was diagnosed with atrial fibrillation in her 30s.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain on exertion and the episode was not brought on by exercise or emotional stress
trigger_question_examples: Do you get chest pain when you exert yourself?
Was there any exercise or strong emotion right before it happened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't get chest pain when I exercise, and I wasn't doing anything active or upset — I was just sitting there.
